title: Fra kursfald til comeback? Investorer peger på to interes­sante C25-aktier
summary_md:
# Fra kursfald til comeback? Investorer peger på to interes­sante C25-aktier

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-04-29  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A Danish Børsen investing podcast examining which C25 companies can actually generate returns (ROIC) in a structurally weak Danish equity market starved of fund inflows. The hosts dissect medtech names Ambu and Coloplast, growth-challenged Novo Nordisk, turnaround stories Vestas and Pandora, and stress capital discipline plus aggressive buybacks as the key value driver, before interviewing Trophy Games' CEO about its top-rated small-cap gaming case.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 VWS — Vestas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Wind turbine turnaround from low earnings base; asset-light, net cash, ROIC up to 40% in good periods; broader wind market improving (Nordex, GE Vernova).

#### Reasoning

Ole is bullish on Vestas as a major C25 turnaround with 11% expected annual earnings growth off a low base, making targets easier to hit. He cites strong economics in good periods (ROIC ~40%), asset-light model and net cash. He targets 220-250 by year-end and 350-400 within 4-5 years. Peter remains cautious due to dependence on public/political subsidy decisions.

#### Sell Triggers

- if public/political subsidy support weakens materially, especially in the US
- if the broader wind turbine market deteriorates again
- if gross margin stays stuck near 15%

> *"Jag tror i alla fall 220 till 250, och så har jag regnat... så är det rätt nejmt att regna en 350-400 om en 4-5 år."*

---

### 🟢 MIPS — MIPS | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Followed for years; stock collapsed from 1200 to ~220. Ole accumulated at avg ~240 (now ~280) after waiting patiently for the price he wanted.

#### Reasoning

After discussing MIPS with Peter for years, Ole became a shareholder, buying around 220-240 average price following the steep decline from 1200. His approach is to wait patiently for the right valuation and then act decisively, fitting his discipline of buying good businesses when they fall into his return-on-capital screen.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the underlying business deteriorates
- if return on invested capital fails to recover

> *"Peter och jag har diskuterat en MIPS i lång tid, och nu är jag auktionär."*

---

### 🟠 AMBU-B — Ambu | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Analysts expect ~18% annual earnings growth for 10y, but stock keeps falling on weak Danish-equity appetite; hosts find valuation increasingly interesting.

#### Reasoning

Ambu is no longer the most expensive C25 name and has become markedly better at making money. Peter would consider buying once the valuation reaches a level that makes sense and wants more clarity from the late-May report; Ole also finds it interesting if execution improves. Both flag that high growth estimates are easy to make and hard to deliver, and trust must be rebuilt after 7-8 prior downgrades.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the late-May report disappoints
- if the company issues further guidance downgrades
- if ~18% growth proves unrealistic to execute

> *"Jag syns faktiskt, att Ambu ser intressant ut. Vi ska se dem här i slutningen av maj månad när de har rapporterat."*

---

### 🟠 PNDORA — Pandora | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Jewelry name down on higher silver prices and fashion-product risk; new CEO; Peter sees attractive valuation and little growth priced in, waiting for next quarter.

#### Reasoning

Peter has followed Pandora and finds the valuation attractive after the decline, arguing little of the future growth is reflected in the price. He wants to watch the next quarter and the new CEO before buying, and notes concerns over silver prices and the move away from silver in production, plus its fashion-driven product risk.

#### Sell Triggers

- if next quarter's results disappoint
- if silver/precious metal prices rise sharply
- if the new CEO changes strategy unfavorably
- if buybacks are paused longer

> *"Jag syns att Pandora är intressant... aktien har kommit ner i ett nivå hvor den ser fin ut på värdeintagelsen."*

---

### 🟡 COLO-B — Coloplast | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Guidance cut on US reimbursement changes plus 3bn Kerecis writedown; trades 18-19x earnings on ~5-6% top-line growth, not cheap despite fall from 1200 to 400.

#### Reasoning

Peter, a shareholder (bought ~457, down ~18%), views the guidance cut as minor and largely Kerecis-related, and the writedown as a way to reset the balance sheet for the incoming CEO and improve future ROIC. But with ROIC fallen to ~14-15% and the stock still at 18-19x earnings on modest growth, it is not a clear bargain; the market needs evidence of earnings lift from acquisitions before re-rating.

#### Sell Triggers

- if US wound-care reimbursement worsens further
- if acquisitions fail to lift earnings per share
- if ROIC does not recover toward the 15% screen level

> *"aktien är inte superbillig efter att den har fallit från 1200 till 400."*

---

### 🟡 NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Huge earnings machine but growth worries; guided negative sales growth, PE ~11, dividend ~5%; long-term growth estimate only 3.3%/yr; GLP market size uncertain.

#### Reasoning

Peter is not worried about Novo's ability to earn (~100bn profit) but about growth—near-term negative guidance plus uncertainty after US/Europe patent expiry around 2031-32. ROIC fell from ~80% to 40% after Catalent. At PE ~11 it is historically cheap, but with flat-to-low earnings growth it is not necessarily a bargain; a re-rating requires evidence of earnings progress. GLP market sizing remains highly uncertain.

#### Sell Triggers

- if GLP/obesity market proves smaller than hoped
- if a large dilutive acquisition pressures ROIC further
- if earnings growth stays flat
- generic competition (semaglutide) eroding pricing

> *"Jag är mycket mer bekymrad för växten... En PE på 11. Men om intjäningsutvecklingen är flad, så är det ju inte nödvändigtvis speciellt billigt."*

---

### 🟡 TEVA — Teva | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Ole's exception to his ROIC screen, a bet on the CEO's value-creation; outstanding result after a long wait, bought when stock was washed out.

#### Reasoning

Ole made a deliberate exception to his 15% ROIC screen for Teva, betting on CEO Kåre Schultz's ability to generate value. After years of looking poor it turned strongly positive and ran hard once it caught on; he was fortunate to buy when the stock was heavily sold off. He continues to hold the exception.

#### Sell Triggers

- if management's value-creation stalls
- if the turnaround momentum reverses

> *"det har varit framragande med Teva exempelvis. Det tog bara lång tid för att den liksom begynte att komma iväg."*

---
